In-browser glass render vs Cycles path-traced truth · report 046

A cheap, deployable screen-space transmission shader (grab the backdrop → GPU mip-pyramid roughness-blur driven by haze σs∝h → tint by transmittance T), fed the ground-truth material maps, rendered live in WebGL2. Compare it to the Cycles path-traced truth. This is the same technique as three.js MeshPhysicalMaterial (transmission / roughness / attenuation) — no path tracing. Numbers are 8-bit sRGB MAE, computed live in-page at 256².

view angle 0°
At angle 0 the panels show the ceiling vs Cycles truth. Tilt the view: T1/cos is the absorption path‑length correction (a global factor — a per‑pixel thickness map is algebraically identical, buys nothing). Slab parallax is the one thickness‑dependent effect — lower σs to see it warp the checker. No Cycles truth exists at grazing (the model is a flat surface BSDF), so the diff then shows the thickness contribution.

Verdict

Cycles truthpath-traced
Browser shaderMAE –
Diff ×3|render − truth|

Rigorous ceiling is measured in numpy at 1024² against the same maps and is byte-identical in metric to oracle 045; this interactive prototype runs at 256² for embed size, so its live MAE tracks the ceiling closely but not exactly. Refraction and veil are toggles: oracle 045 found relief-refraction marginal (<0.15 MAE) and the front veil identically zero in a backlit rig — toggling veil ON here adds error, which is the point (it belongs to a future front-lit scene, not this one).
